perturbation.original_text: 1.8 "Dassault Systemes Customer(s)" means any customer of the Co-Branded Service having accessed such Web service, wherever hosted, by first logging in on any Dassault Systemes Web site.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.8 "Dassault Systemes User(s)" means any individual accessing the Co-Branded Service, irrespective of whether they are customers of Dassault Systemes or not.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Dassault Systemes Customer(s)' is changed to 'Dassault Systemes User(s)' making it broader. Initially, it referred specifically to customers accessing the Co-Branded Service through a Dassault Systemes Web site. The change now encompasses any individual, customer or not, accessing the service. This creates a contradiction when the term is later used in sections related to revenue sharing based on 'customers', because revenue calculations will be based on all Users, instead of the initial customers. This change has financial consequences as revenue sharing becomes affected by non-customers.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As financial consideration under this Agreement, Net Revenue will be shared by the parties as follows: (a) For so long as PlanetCAD hosts the Co-Branded Service, during which time PlanetCAD shall be the Billing Party, all Net Revenue derived from sales of the Co-Branded Service to Dassault Systemes Customers shall be apportioned [***] percent ([***]%) to Dassault Systems and [***] percent ([***]%) to PlanetCAD. (b) In the event that Dassault Systemes opts to host the Co-Branded Service pursuant to Section 4.7 [Change of Hosting] herein they shall become the Billing Party and all Net Revenue derived from sales of the Co-Branded Service shall be apportioned [***] percent ([***]%) to Dassault Systems and [***] percent ([***]%) to PlanetCAD.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Dassault Systemes User(s)' broadens the scope from 'Dassault Systemes Customers' to include any individual accessing the service, regardless of customer status. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which specifies that revenue sharing is based on sales to 'Dassault Systemes Customers'. Consequently, the revenue calculations would now include revenue derived from non-customers, altering the financial consideration and creating a direct contradiction with the original intent of the revenue-sharing agreement. This is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, creating conflicting obligations as to which revenue should be shared.

perturbation.original_text: 1.11 "Dassault Systemes Web sites" means any Web site created, owned, operated or supported by or for Dassault Systemes.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.11 "Dassault Systemes Digital Assets" means any digital platform, application, or online presence controlled, affiliated, or utilized by Dassault Systemes.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Dassault Systemes Web sites' is changed to 'Dassault Systemes Digital Assets.' The original definition limited the scope to just websites. The modified version broadens it to include any digital platform, application or online presence. This creates an inconsistency in Section 4.3 where the agreement specifies how the Co-Branded Service will be accessed from 'Dassault Systemes Web site(s).' Now, with the new definition, it is not clear if the link should be available on all 'Digital Assets,' potentially expanding PlanetCAD's obligations beyond the initial scope and causing confusion about the access points.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Term of this Agreement, the Co-Branded Service will be accessible from any Dassault Systemes Web site(s), as determined by Dassault Systemes in its sole discretion, on a fully transparent basis by way of a direct link to the first Web page of the Co-Branded Service. Upon exiting the Co-Branded Service, the Dassault Systemes Customers will be automatically returned to the Dassault Systemes Web site. PlanetCAD shall, with consultation and input from Dassault Systemes, perform all services necessary for the implementation of such link between the Dassault Systemes Web site and the Co-Branded Service.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Dassault Systemes Web sites' narrowly. The changed text broadens the definition to 'Dassault Systemes Digital Assets.' This creates a contradiction with the contradicted text, which specifies access to the Co-Branded Service from 'Dassault Systemes Web site(s).' The broader definition now makes it unclear whether the link should be available on all 'Digital Assets,' potentially expanding PlanetCAD's obligations beyond the initial scope, fulfilling the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation definition.

perturbation.original_text: 1.27 "Server Software" means all software, including without limitation, Web interface, Web middleware, Web dynamic content billing, Web content generation software, and any Derivative Works thereof that is used by PlanetCAD to provide services on the Co-Branded Service. The list of all such Software as of the Effective Date is attached in Schedule A which will be updated by PlanetCAD when reasonably requested by Dassault Systemes and when in PlanetCAD's determination significant changes have been made.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.27 "Core Infrastructure" means all technologies, including, but not limited to web interfaces, middleware, billing systems, content creation tools, and their derivatives used by PlanetCAD to support the Co-Branded Service. Schedule A lists the initial components, subject to updates only when mandated by regulatory requirements.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Server Software' is changed to 'Core Infrastructure,' and its update mechanism is altered. Originally, PlanetCAD was responsible for updating Schedule A reflecting significant changes to the software components, as reasonably requested by Dassault Systemes. The modified version restricts updates to 'Core Infrastructure' listings only to regulatory requirements. This leads to an ambiguity in Section 3.3 because 'Server Software' is licensed to Dassault Systemes. The contradiction arises as Dassault Systemes no longer has assurance that the 'Server Software' listed, now called 'Core Infrastructure,' will be kept up-to-date with the latest enhancements, potentially affecting service performance and creating a legal loophole if updates are needed, but not mandated by regulations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PlanetCAD hereby grants to Dassault Systemes a fully-paid, non-exclusive, worldwide, revocable limited license to the Server Software and Infrastructure for the sole purpose of (i) hosting the Co-Branded Service and (ii) fulfilling its obligations under this Agreement. The license granted pursuant to this Section 3.3 [Limited Hosting License] shall be deemed to be automatically revoked upon termination of this Agreement. Within 2 weeks following execution of this Agreement, PlanetCAD will provide Dassault Systemes with the necessary media and licensing keys or similar authorization system (if any) to use such Server Software and Infrastructure according to the above license.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Server Software' to 'Core Infrastructure' and the restriction on updates to Schedule A (only when mandated by regulatory requirements) directly contradicts Section 3.3. Section 3.3 grants Dassault Systemes a license to 'Server Software' and Infrastructure. The original definition of 'Server Software' allowed Dassault Systemes to request updates, ensuring the licensed software remained current. The new definition of 'Core Infrastructure' removes this right, restricting updates to regulatory mandates only. This creates a strong contradiction because Dassault Systemes no longer has assurance the 'Server Software' they are licensed to use will be updated, potentially impacting their ability to fulfill obligations under the agreement, leading to potential disputes. The term 'Server Software' becomes inconsistent with 'Core Infrastructure' as referenced in Section 3.3.
